specs.key_requirements: R2: Generator state machine compilation — yield → suspend, state enum (Created/Running/Suspended/Completed), R3: Generator send/throw/close protocol — full CPython semantics, R4: yield from delegation — sub-iterator forwarding, return value passthrough, R5: Custom iterator protocol — __iter__/__next__ MRO dispatch via mb_iter_new/mb_iter_next, Conformance test harness pattern: .py + .expected golden file, mamba-xfail directive

Conformance fixture structure: tests/fixtures/conformance/{category}/{name}.py + .expected, Test runner: cargo test -p cclab-mamba --test conformance_tests, Fixture categories: generators/, iterators/, data_structures/, language/, builtins/, Scenario format: GIVEN/WHEN/THEN for conformance verification

R1: GeneratorState enum (Created→Running→Suspended→Completed) — generator state machine, R2: yield suspension/resumption — local variables preserved in heap frame, R3: Generator as iterator — __iter__ returns self, __next__ resumes until yield/return, R4: send(value) — yield expression evaluates to sent value; first send must be None, R5: throw(exc)/close() — inject exception at yield point; GeneratorExit on close

R1: mb_iter_new/mb_iter_next — call __iter__/__next__ on target, StopIteration terminates loop, R2: Built-in iterator variants — ListIter, DictIter, SetIter, TupleIter with index tracking, R3: Iterator combinators — enumerate(start), zip(*iters), reversed(seq), filter(fn), map(fn), R4: range(stop/start/stop/step) — integer range with __len__ support, R5: StopIteration exhaustion — next(iter, default) returns default instead of raising

R1: Mutation — append, extend, insert, pop(i?), remove, clear (all 6 mutation methods), R2: Query — index(x), count(x), copy (non-mutating, all 3 query methods), R3: sort(key?, reverse?) — in-place sort with optional key function; reverse() in-place, R4: Subscript/slicing — getitem/setitem/delitem with negative index; slice(start:stop:step), R5: Construction — mb_list_new(), mb_list_from_values() for literal compilation

R1: Lookup — get(key, default=None), setdefault(key, default=None), R2: Views — keys(), values(), items() returning materialized lists, R3: Mutation — update(other), pop(key, default?), clear(), copy(), R4: Subscript — getitem/setitem/delitem; KeyError on missing key, R5: PEP 584 merge — mb_dict_merge for {**d1, **d2} unpacking expressions

R1: Element management — add, remove (KeyError), discard (no error), pop, R2: Queries — contains (in operator), len, R3: Set algebra — union (|), intersection (&), difference (-), symmetric_difference (^), R4: Comparisons — issubset (<=), issuperset (>=), R5: NaN-boxed u64 hashing — raw bit patterns, clear as utility method

R1: 23 core methods — split, join, strip, lstrip, rstrip, replace, find, rfind, startswith, endswith, upper, lower, title, capitalize, count, swapcase, expandtabs, partition, rpartition, removeprefix, removesuffix, maketrans, translate, R2: 6 predicates — isdigit, isalpha, isalnum, isspace, isupper, islower, R3: Alignment — center, ljust, rjust, zfill, R4: F-string runtime formatting — mb_string_format(template, args, argc), R5: Encoding — mb_string_encode(s, encoding) → ObjData::Bytes

R1: Iterator builtins — enumerate(iterable, start=0), zip(iter_a, iter_b), reversed(seq), R2: Aggregation — min(iter), max(iter), sum(iter, start=0), sorted(iter), R3: Type checking — isinstance(obj, classinfo) via MRO walk, R4: Conversion — repr, hash, id, input, chr, ord, hex, oct, bin, R5: P1 builtins — abs, all, any, divmod, filter, map, round (20 additional functions), R6: Symbol registration — all mb_builtin_* registered in symbols.rs with correct MirExtern signatures

R5: Tuple ordering — mb_tuple_lt/le/gt/ge for lexicographic comparison, Lexicographic algorithm: element-by-element, shorter tuple is less when all compared equal, Integration with mb_values_lt/le/gt/ge dispatch in builtins.rs

R1: RUN directive dispatch — parse, typecheck, jit runners, R2: Enhanced error reporting — file path + diagnostic engine context in failures, R3: Recursive fixture discovery — auto-discovers .py files under tests/fixtures/, R4: CPython fixture directory — tests/fixtures/parse/cpython/ for parse-only checks, R5: Directive-based format — # RUN: parse at top of each fixture

CPython compliance test in tests/cpython_compat.rs with datatest-stable harness, cpython_known_failures.toml xfail manifest for expected parse failures, Fixture path: tests/fixtures/cpython/ for CPython source snippets, xpass warning when previously-failing fixture now passes

R1: Type-tagged dispatch — mb_call_method routes by ObjData variant tag, R2: MRO fallback — ObjData::Instance falls back to C3 MRO attribute lookup, R5: Magic method dispatch — __len__, __contains__, __iter__, __repr__, __str__, R8: Context manager protocol — __enter__/__exit__ dispatch for with-statement, Required for custom iterator protocol test fixtures
Background: stdlib module coverage targets (argparse, platform, unittest, socket, etc.), Test strategy: inline #[cfg(test)] + integration tests for per-function branch coverage

Background: lowest-coverage stdlib modules (queue, statistics, shlex, calendar, locale, lzma, zlib, secrets, bisect, abc), Self-contained test strategy: no network, no filesystem writes outside temp dirs
